name: codebase-understanding
description: Answers questions about a codebase using source-grounded evidence. Every factual claim must be traceable to a specific file and line.

input.description: A natural-language question about the target codebase.

fields.name: question
fields.description: The question to answer about the codebase.

output.description: Structured JSON with a status, an answer, source-grounded claims, and the tool calls used.

tools.name: repo_search
tools.description: Case-insensitive substring search across the codebase. Returns {file, line, text} matches.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a file from the codebase by repo-relative path.

citationRequirement: Every claim must include at least one citation of the form {file, line}. The cited line must contain a keyword from the claim.
unsupportedClaimPolicy: If the skill answers, every claim must have a valid citation. Claims that cannot be grounded must be dropped and the status set to insufficient_evidence rather than invented.
failureBehavior: When evidence is ambiguous or missing, return status=insufficient_evidence with an empty claims array. Never fabricate citations or symbols.
validationRules: Output must match the required JSON schema.
Every citation must reference an existing file and an in-range line number.
Each cited line must support its claim (shared keyword).
When status is answered, every required symbol must appear on a cited line and every expected file must be cited.
Forbidden/hallucinated claims from the test case must not appear in the output.
Required tools must be called, and repo_search must precede read_file.
